codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex invocation blocked by pretool-bash-safety.sh pattern-match on prompt text containing 'git branch -D' (the very substring the ticket is fixing). Exit code 2, BLOCKED: direct git ref mutation. This is the third independent observation of the same hook behavior — confirming the BA's diagnosis. Self-review substituted per graceful-fallback contract.
codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: self-review substituted — five adversarial questions reviewed: (1) root cause attribution confirmed correct; (2) proposed sentinel check in _check_git_allowlist confirmed as minimal sufficient fix; (3) subagent firewall correctly excluded from sentinel path per M2; (4) force-push guard preserved by _evaluate_push call ordering; (5) no scope drift found.

findings.title: Root cause confirmed by direct source read — file and line citations verified
findings.detail: hooks/pretool-git-privilege-guard.py:144-155 confirmed: _check_git_allowlist() calls only read_grant_for_git_command(command, sid) — no call to match_sentinel_grant_for_bash_command(). Import line 92 confirms only read_grant_for_git_command is imported from lib.allowlist. hooks/lib/allowlist.py:438 confirms match_sentinel_grant_for_bash_command(task_id: str, command: str) -> dict | None exists with the correct signature. BA evidence is direct and precise.

findings.title: M2 design decision confirmed in pretool-bash-safety.sh source
findings.detail: pretool-bash-safety.sh:483-486 contains comment: 'M2 (task 20260521-090200): sentinel check extended to IS_SUBAGENT=1 so user-created structured grants are honored in subagent context. The IS_SUBAGENT guard at line 145 (legacy pattern-string grants) REMAINS UNCHANGED.' This confirms the BA's claim that the M2 decision is already implemented in bash-safety.sh and must be mirrored in the privilege guard.

findings.title: User requirement R3 universalist clause ('No exceptions. User consent is sovereign.') is fully addressed
findings.detail: The BA correctly maps R3 to AC2 (subagent firewall does NOT suppress sentinel check) and AC5 (legacy firewall preserved). The fix covers all agent contexts: main-agent with sentinel (AC1), subagent with sentinel (AC2), and both contexts without grant (AC3, AC5). No scope narrowing detected.

findings.title: Force-push guard ordering verified by QA — BA constraint correct
findings.detail: QA independently verified: _evaluate_push() calls _push_has_forbidden_ref_mutation(command) at line 610 BEFORE _check_git_allowlist(command, data) at line 617. _evaluate_command() fast-path at line 695 excludes push commands with 'if not _looks_like_git_push(command)'. Therefore a sentinel for 'git push -f' cannot bypass the force-push block — the function calls _block() with sys.exit(2) before reaching _check_git_allowlist. BA constraint 'Do not alter the force-push ordering in _evaluate_push()' is correctly stated and enforced by the current code structure.

findings.title: All BA-cited file paths and line numbers verified to exist and contain claimed code
findings.detail: Verified: hooks/pretool-git-privilege-guard.py:92 (import), :144 (_check_git_allowlist def), :150 (agent_id check), :155 (read_grant_for_git_command call), :610 (_push_has_forbidden_ref_mutation), :617 (_check_git_allowlist in _evaluate_push). hooks/lib/allowlist.py:179 (read_grant_for_git_command def), :438 (match_sentinel_grant_for_bash_command def). hooks/pretool-bash-safety.sh:483 (M2 comment), :508 (match_sentinel call), :521 (SENTINEL_OK exit 0). hooks/userprompt-consent-allowlist.sh:183 (TASK_ID assignment). All citations are accurate.

findings.title: AC fixture data matches actual sentinel writer output — no drift
findings.detail: QA traced the sentinel writer (userprompt-consent-allowlist.sh:192-214): for input '/allow git branch -D foo', parts=['git','branch -D foo'], op='git', rest='branch -D foo', entry={'op':'git','args_contain':['branch -D foo']}. AC1/AC2 fixture specifies {op:'git', args_contain:['branch -D foo']}. These match exactly. QA also traced match_sentinel_grant_for_bash_command() for command 'git branch -D foo': tokens=['git','branch','-D','foo'], head_op='git', rest='branch -D foo', args_contain check: 'branch -D foo' in 'branch -D foo' = True. Match succeeds. AC fixture is correct.

findings.title: AC8 check command is a stub — manual grep is sufficient, bash_assertion is fine
findings.detail: AC8 check.type is 'bash_assertion' with a python AST command that only prints 'OK' and a note 'Manual grep: line number of _push_has_forbidden_ref_mutation call < line number of _check_git_allowlist call'. QA verified this ordering directly: _push_has_forbidden_ref_mutation at line 610, _check_git_allowlist at line 617. The AC is verifiable even if the command itself is a stub — the assertion is checkable with grep or a proper AST line-comparison. Minor presentational gap; no drift requiring BA spec update.
findings.recommendation: Dev should implement AC8 as a real assertion: assert line_of(_push_has_forbidden_ref_mutation) < line_of(_check_git_allowlist) within _evaluate_push body, using ast.walk. The current stub passes by default.

findings.title: subagent SID mismatch edge case (Edge Case 3) acknowledged but not resolved by BA
findings.detail: BA Edge Case 3: subagents carry a different session_id than the orchestrator. The proposed task_id resolution uses CLAUDE_TASK_ID env var with SID fallback. If CLAUDE_TASK_ID is not set in the subagent env, the fallback is data['session_id'] — which differs from the orchestrator's SID used as the fallback in the writer. This means a sentinel written by the orchestrator (task_id=orchestrator_SID) would not be found by a subagent using task_id=subagent_SID. BA marks this as 'dev must verify' — appropriate for a first-cycle spec. QA concurs: the M2 bash-safety.sh implementation at lines 487-494 has the same fallback pattern, so the risk is already accepted at the architecture level.

dimension_summary.evidence_quality: PASS — root cause confirmed by source read with file:line citations; BA did not make unsupported claims; diagnosis_completeness is honestly marked 'incomplete — inferred from source read' which is accurate and appropriate.
dimension_summary.scope_alignment: PASS — user requirement is 'No exceptions. User consent is sovereign.' BA's Must Have maps to all three R1/R2/R3 clauses; ACs 1-5 cover main-agent sentinel, subagent sentinel, no-grant fallback, legacy-main-agent, and legacy-subagent firewall. No scope narrowing or renaming detected.
dimension_summary.investigation_completeness: PASS — requirement is a hook fix, not an audit. BA investigated the actual code path (_check_git_allowlist call chain), identified the missing call, traced the reference implementation in bash-safety.sh, and verified the library function signature. No investigation deliverable was skipped.
dimension_summary.affected_file_accuracy: PASS — all file paths verified to exist; all cited line numbers verified to contain the claimed code; import chain from pretool-git-privilege-guard.py through lib/allowlist.py confirmed correct.
dimension_summary.spec_text_vs_execution_drift: PASS — ACs specify pytest tests with direct function calls. AC fixture data matches actual sentinel writer output. AC8 uses a stub check command but the assertion is manually verifiable and does not require BA spec update. No meaningful drift detected.

analysis.concern: Sentinel bypasses subagent firewall for git ref-mutation operations
analysis.assessment: INTENTIONAL — this is the M2 design decision from task 20260521-090200, already implemented in pretool-bash-safety.sh. Sentinels are written only by /allow (userprompt-consent-allowlist.sh), which has its own subagent write-firewall at line 17-23 (only main-agent can write sentinels). The sentinel is the user's explicit consent mechanism. Extending it to subagents is the stated goal.

NOT POSSIBLE — _evaluate_push() calls _push_has_forbidden_ref_mutation() at line 610 BEFORE _check_git_allowlist() at line 617. The _evaluate_command() fast-path at line 695 excludes push with 'if not _looks_like_git_push(command)'. Force-push calls _block(sys.exit(2)) before any sentinel check is reached.

analysis.concern: Sentinel over-match — 'git branch -D foo' sentinel matching unrelated git subcommands
analysis.assessment: NOT POSSIBLE — sentinel entry is {op:'git', args_contain:['branch -D foo']}. match_sentinel_grant_for_bash_command checks args_contain as substring of rest (tokens[1:]). For 'git status', rest='status'; 'branch -D foo' is not in 'status'. No over-match. AC6 explicitly tests this.

blast_radius_assessment.change_type: additive — new allow path, no existing block rules weakened
blast_radius_assessment.band_aid_check: CLEAN — proposed fix adds a new check, does not weaken or remove any existing check. Legacy grant path and subagent firewall for legacy path are explicitly preserved.
blast_radius_assessment.regression_risk: low — change is additive; all existing block paths are preserved; sentinel path follows the same pattern already proven in pretool-bash-safety.sh

overall_assessment: BA specification is well-formed, accurately diagrams the root cause, correctly identifies the fix, and provides verifiable acceptance criteria. All 5 validation dimensions pass. The fix is minimal (one file, additive change), consistent with the M2 design decision already implemented in the parallel hook, and security-reviewed with no regressions identified. Advisory note: dev should implement AC8 as a real AST assertion rather than a stub, and must verify CLAUDE_TASK_ID propagation to subagents (Edge Case 3).
